name: Neurolief — Correctional Mental Health (DEFERRED)
description: Correctional systems (state DOCs, large jail systems) and the correctional-healthcare contractors that run their mental health services, where there is a publicly documented, robust mental health program. SECONDARY, lower-priority ICP — longer government procurement cycle, bespoke sourcing. Signals come from government program pages, court-ordered settlements, contractor announcements, and procurement records — NOT practice-website scraping. Do not assign this profile to a run without first building the public-record source adapter.
scoring_notes: Buyer is a DOC or a correctional-healthcare contractor (e.g. Centurion, Wexford, YesCare, VitalCore), not a practice. Strict public-source anchoring is non-negotiable. state_mandate_status does not apply.

signals.prompt_instruction: Is there a publicly documented mental health program at this correctional system — for example a state Department of Corrections mental health program page, a named psychiatric inpatient or outpatient program, or a court-ordered mental health settlement (e.g. a Coleman-type consent decree)?
signals.note: QUALIFIER. Must be backed by a government or court public source. Must-have for Bullseye tier.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does public documentation indicate the correctional system already delivers mental health care via telepsychiatry or telehealth?
signals.note: Strong fit signal. A system already using remote-supervised, low-infrastructure care is structurally ready for the Proliv Rx delivery model.

signals.prompt_instruction: Is the mental health service operated by an identifiable correctional-healthcare contractor (for example Centurion, Wexford Health, YesCare, VitalCore, or similar), per a public contract, award, or announcement?
signals.note: The contractor is frequently the actual buyer. Public contract or award documentation required.

signals.prompt_instruction: Is there public documentation of the psychiatric caseload, depression prevalence, or scale of the mental health population at this system (e.g. published census, audit, or settlement reporting)?
signals.note: Scale evidence supports the size of the addressable TRD population. Public reporting only.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does available public information indicate the facility has no documented mental health program, or is mental health fully contracted out with no public signal to anchor on?
signals.note: EXCLUSIONARY direction. A confirmed 'no program' reading heavily penalizes; absence of evidence is not_found, not a penalty.
